title: Fooled by Randomness
subtitle: The Hidden Role of Chance in Life and in the Markets
author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
themes: Humans systematically mistake luck for skill
Survivorship bias distorts our view of success
Gaussian distributions fail to capture real-world risk
We construct narratives to explain random events
Alternative histories reveal the role of chance
The problem of induction makes prediction unreliable
Epistemic humility is the only rational response to uncertainty
awards: Fortune 75 Smartest Books of All Time
First book in the Incerto series
excerpt: We mistake luck for skill because we only see the winners — the losers are invisible. A trader with a perfect ten-year record may simply be the one survivor out of a thousand who never flipped tails. This book is a devastating tour of how chance, probability, and randomness fool us in markets and in life, and why the most dangerous illusion is the belief that we understand what we cannot predict.
